title: Guzmania ekmanii
intro: Guzmania ekmanii, also known as the Ecuadorian airplant, is a species of epiphytic bromeliad, characterized by its vibrant green leaves and vibrant flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Guzmania ekmanii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Guzmania ekmanii is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used as a tincture, to treat respiratory conditions, such as bronchitis and asthma.
taxonomy: Guzmania ekmanii is a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. Guzmania ekmanii is classified as a genus within the Bromeliad family, which is a group of monocotyledonous plants native to the tropical regions of the Americas. The classification of Guzmania ekmanii is further narrowed down to the subfamily Tillandsioideae and tribe Tillandsieae. This species is part of the genus Guzmania, which is characterized by its epiphytic habit and distinctive inflorescence. Guzmania ekmanii is a relatively rare species, and its classification is an important aspect of its identification and conservation.
